title: ConvertKit vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and GetResponse: pricing, features, ease of use, and which email marketing platform suits your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs GetResponse: Which Email Marketing Platform Wins?
intro: Both ConvertKit and GetResponse are popular email marketing platforms designed for creators, course sellers, and online entrepreneurs. While they share similar core functionality, they differ significantly in pricing, feature depth, and specialization. This comparison will help you decide which platform best fits your business needs and budget.
choose_a_if: You prioritize ease of use and a creator-focused interface with visual automation builders
You need e-commerce integration and product selling capabilities out of the box
You want a higher free tier limit (1,000 subscribers) to test before upgrading
choose_b_if: You need webinar hosting and online course hosting built into one platform
You want the lowest entry price and most comprehensive feature set for the cost
You prefer a platform with longer industry experience and higher Trustpilot ratings

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit excels for creators with simpler workflows and e-commerce needs, offering an intuitive interface and strong community. GetResponse is better if you need webinars, online courses, and advanced automation all in one platform. ConvertKit scores slightly higher on G2 (4.4 vs 4.2), but GetResponse rates better on Trustpilot (3.7 vs 3.2).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or GetResponse?
faq.a: GetResponse is more affordable overall, starting at $19/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. GetResponse also offers a higher free plan ($0 vs $0), though with fewer subscribers (500 vs 1,000). For budget-conscious users, GetResponse typically provides more value for the price, especially if you need multiple features like webinars and courses.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your email list from GetResponse to ConvertKit. Both platforms support CSV imports and have documented migration processes. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your subscriber lists, though you'll need to manually recreate automations and sequences in the new platform. Always back up your data before switching.
